reviews.review_msg: Professor Mark is really passionate about his teachings and will be there to guide you with writing essays. Go to office hours if you need clarification on how he graded your paper, it helped me become a better writer.

That was a super challenging class and he is a tough marker. Having the chance to write your papers is really, really helpful but it doesn't get easier. He asks complex questions and the exam was really challenging. I would recommend this class if you want to get better at writing and are down to go to office hours. go to office hours!!

Mark is a decent prof, the topics he teaches about are usually very abstract so it's kind of hard to grasp what he's trying to say during the lectures. there are lots of readings it's hard to keep up! make sure to look at the reading list and make sure the books are to your liking. you'll need to write a couple essays/paragraphs for the final exam

Mark is super passionate about the class, and is actually interested in seeing you learn and do well. Most books needed are online and free. He offers a lot of extra help outside of class via in person or zoom. Was very Understanding if you need extra time on work for a good reason.

The online course isn't set up very well, unclear grading criteria and deadlines/grades weren't getting pushed out adhering to course syllabus. Assignment deadlines kept getting pushed back. Mediocre course to take as a GPA booster.

Mark is a very nice prof! He is very passionate about the subject and tries to make the content interesting for students. The main concepts seem big, scary & abstract but he simplifies it for you. He also gives good feedback on the assignments and allows you to rewrite them to improve your grade. He is always there to help & chat.

Ken is an average professor. Would rate him 2.5/5. His class is easy to pass. However, I found him subjective in terms of grading. He makes up his mind the first time he sees you which is impossible to change. He is also a bit more on the moody type. So walk on eggshells around him and don't offend him.

Didn't realize it before since it was in my clueless first year but after finishing his course, it was quite good. I got a lot of information from it and also really enjoyed the analysis from his chosen novels. Love his lectures, very concise and insightful. Wish he was back at SFU would love to take another WL course with him again.

Greg is a wonderful professor and person in general. If you have a chance to take a course with him DO IT. He is funny, engaging, and highly inspiring. An all around cool guy. He's the reason I majored in World Lit

He's an amazing teacher.  He's knowledgeable, involved, hilarious, and treats every single student with respect and gives them positive feedback for offering opinions in class.  The material was excellent, I learned so much; but at the same time failed another course because of the workload for this one. There was so much to do! Not an easy course

Greg is probably one of the best profs/teachers I've ever had.  I learnt alot about literature and it's relevance to the outside world as well as myself. I hope that he comes back to do more World Lit courses at SFU.
